Some people say that just seeing something is not enough, because our eyes can lie to us. But even then, we must check if our own eyes are working well. If many people see the same bird, and they also see it, then the idea that it is a bird has a better chance of being true. So, a good reason must come from things that are hard to get to, not just from what we feel inside. So my answer is: a good reason is one that comes from what we can see, hear, or touch, because it must come from the world we live in and see for our own persons.
